see_evidence: PMC: students treat fractions as 2 independent whole numbers — 2/6 > 1/2 because 2>1 and 6>2 (Stafylidou & Vosniadou 2004)
EdWeek 2024: most common number line error — 3/8 > 3/4 because 8>4, do not understand piece size
PMC intervention: divide number line into denominator segments; larger denominator = closer to zero
Riccomini 2025: error analysis integrated into intervention is cornerstone of effective math intervention (NCII)
PMC SDBF-OR: smallest denominator seen as biggest fraction without considering numerator

behavioral_signals.label: Question abandonment
behavioral_signals.teacher_action: Student may be stuck — offer hint

conceptual_signals.label: Whole number bias
conceptual_signals.description: Treats fraction as two independent whole numbers
conceptual_signals.detection: Larger numerator or denominator always placed as greater
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Use unit fraction anchor: show 1/2 vs 1/6 with equal-length segments

conceptual_signals.label: Denominator size confusion
conceptual_signals.description: Believes larger denominator means larger fraction
conceptual_signals.detection: Fraction with larger denominator consistently placed higher
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Pizza model: 8 slices vs 4 slices — which piece is bigger?

conceptual_signals.label: Tick mark not interval counting
conceptual_signals.description: Counts tick marks instead of spaces on number line
conceptual_signals.detection: Correct denominator but one position off
conceptual_signals.example: 1/4 placed at first tick mark instead of after first interval
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Explicitly teach: count the spaces not the lines. Use colored interval blocks.

conceptual_signals.label: Unit fraction magnitude inversion
conceptual_signals.detection: Unit fractions placed in reverse order
conceptual_signals.example: 1/8 placed right of 1/4 on number line
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Fold paper in half vs eighths: which piece is bigger?

conceptual_signals.label: Zero anchor confusion
conceptual_signals.description: Does not anchor fractions between 0 and 1
conceptual_signals.detection: Fractions placed outside 0-1 or wrong side
conceptual_signals.example: Places 3/4 at position 3 on 0-4 number line
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Establish 0 and 1 anchors first. Ask: can this be more than a whole?

conceptual_signals.label: Equivalent fraction non-recognition
conceptual_signals.detection: Places equivalent fractions at different positions
conceptual_signals.example: 1/2 at midpoint but 2/4 at different location
conceptual_signals.teacher_action: Overlay two number lines: halves and fourths. Show alignment.
